name: Trade Balances in Major Countries

book_quotes.quote: Figure 11.2 depicts the trade balances of fifteen major countries from 1960 to 2009. These are measured as the export-import ratio in common currency, so that a ratio greater than one signifies a trade surplus and a ratio below one signifies a trade deficit.
Figure 11.2 Trade Balances in Major Countries, 1960-2009 (Exports/Imports)

Sum of Exports and Imports (EX+IM): From the International Monetary Fund (IMF), International Financial Statistics (IFS), exports and imports in US dollars. Missing values for Belgium for 1960-1992 were taken from AMECO Database, http://ec.europa.eu/economy_finance/ameco/, imports (UMGS) and exports (UXGS) in current prices and units MrdECU/EUR, which were then converted to US dollars using the exchange rate (XNE) in terms of US dollars per ECU/EUR.

primary_source.table_or_series_id: Exports and Imports in US dollars, 15 OECD/major countries, 1960-2009 (with AMECO supplement for Belgium 1960-1992)
primary_source.license: IMF data dissemination terms (public, registration may be required for bulk download)
primary_source.available_through: ShaikhChoppedTables/Appendix11_XMData.xlsx (15 countries x 1960-2009 X, M, X+M, X/M)

formula: Trade balance ratio_{c,t} = X_{c,t} / M_{c,t}, where X and M are total goods exports and imports in current US dollars from IMF IFS; ratio > 1 = surplus, < 1 = deficit.

components.label: US trade balance ratio (S1101-A)
components.source: IMF IFS exports/imports, United States, 1960-2009 (Appendix11_XMData.xlsx row block 16)

components.label: UK trade balance ratio (S1101-B)
components.source: IMF IFS exports/imports, United Kingdom, 1960-2009 (XMData row block 15)

components.label: Germany trade balance ratio (S1101-C)
components.source: IMF IFS exports/imports, Germany, 1960-2009 (XMData row block 7)

components.label: Japan trade balance ratio (S1101-D)
components.source: IMF IFS exports/imports, Japan, 1960-2009 (XMData row block 9)

components.label: China trade balance ratio (S1101-E, illustrative addition)
components.source: Not in Shaikh's Appendix 11.1 sample of 15; CD2 added as comparator. Source: IMF IFS exports/imports, China

extension_candidates.subseries_label: IMF IFS / Direction of Trade Statistics (DOTS) - country exports and imports of goods, current USD, annual
extension_candidates.source: IMF International Financial Statistics (IFS) and Direction of Trade Statistics (DOTS)
concerns: DOTS provides bilateral and total trade in current USD on an ongoing basis; X/M ratio reconstructible identically post-2009.
German reunification break (1991): IFS series for unified Germany begins 1991; pre-1991 series for West Germany must be spliced. Shaikh's XMData already handles this.

extension_candidates.subseries_label: World Bank World Development Indicators - Exports/Imports of goods and services (BoP, current US$)
concerns: WDI includes services; Shaikh's series is goods-only via IFS. Need to separate or accept slight concept drift in extension.

extension_candidates.subseries_label: OECD International Trade Statistics - merchandise trade by country, annual
concerns: OECD coverage for non-OECD countries (e.g., China, Korea pre-1996) more limited than IMF DOTS.

methodology_notes: CD2 predecessor S060 ported: same construction (load XMData, pivot multi-country rows, extract per-country X/M ratio).

Appendix 11.1 specifies 15-country coverage: Australia, Belgium, Canada, Denmark, Finland, France, Germany, Italy, Japan, (Republic of) Korea, Netherlands, Norway, Spain, Sweden, United Kingdom, and United States. China is NOT in Shaikh's original 15; CD2's inclusion of 'China trade balance ratio' (S060-E) appears to be an analyst-added comparator and must be flagged.
Units are common-currency (USD) X/M ratios, not percent-of-GDP. CD2 markdown's 'Percent of GDP' label for S063 is inconsistent with this series' Exports/Imports ratio framing.
Belgium 1960-1992 backfill from AMECO Database (UMGS/UXGS converted via XNE exchange rate). All other countries direct from IMF IFS.

Reference values from Shaikh's published appendix (US example): 1960=1.1988, 1972=0.8358, 1984=0.6467, 1996=0.7604, 2008=0.5997. Match against ShaikhChoppedTables/Appendix11_XMData.xlsx for validation.
open_questions: CD2 added China (S060-E) as a 5th subseries even though Shaikh's Appendix 11.1 lists only 15 OECD countries (no China). Should RSCD retain the China subseries as a useful comparator or drop it to match book scope exactly? Decide in Phase 4 adequacy.
Validation against the digitized XMData ratios for all 15 countries (CD2 cross-checked only US): replicate Shaikh's exact ratios cell-by-cell before declaring source confirmed.
Extension post-2009: which composite (IMF DOTS vs IFS vs WDI) gives the cleanest splice for each of the 15 (+ China?) countries? Phase 6 task.

review_history.notes: CD2 S060 ported; verbatim quotes verified against PDF pp.523-524, 875; Appendix 11.1 source identified as IMF IFS (with AMECO supplement for Belgium).
